title: Thibaut Courtois says Real Madrid future is out of his hands and outlines retirement plan
original_text:
Thibaut Courtois says Real Madrid future is out of his hands and outlines retirement plan
Turkey’s World Cup plans in jeopardy as Real Madrid star suffers significant injury
Real Madrid are concerned that Arda Guler could miss the remainder of the season after suffering a hamstring injury, although there is optimism he could still feature at the World Cup.
The 21-year-old midfielder sustained the issue during Madrid’s recent La Liga fixture against Alaves, where he was forced off midway through the second half.
Advertisement
Subsequent medical tests confirmed a muscle injury in the biceps femoris of his right leg, with the club acknowledging the setback following further assessment.
Early indications suggest the injury is serious enough to rule Guler out for the final weeks of the domestic campaign.
That would represent a significant blow for Madrid as they approach a decisive period in the title race.
Alvaro Arbeloa’s side currently sit second in La Liga and are preparing for a crucial run of fixtures, including a high-stakes El Clasico against Barcelona.
Advertisement
Guler has been an increasingly important part of Madrid’s attacking set-up this season, contributing creativity and goals.
He has made 50 appearances across all competitions, bagging six times and 14 assists.
His absence would remove a key option in midfield and attack during the closing stages of the season.
Despite the setback at club level, there is cautious optimism regarding his availability for the World Cup with Turkey.
Sources close to the player believe he still has enough time to recover before the tournament begins in June.
Advertisement
Guler has been a central figure for Turkey throughout their qualification campaign and is expected to play a leading role if fit.
They secured their place in the tournament following a successful play-off run and will be looking to build around their emerging star.
Madrid are also dealing with a similar concern in defence, with Eder Militao suffering a comparable hamstring injury.
The defender is also expected to miss the remainder of the club season, although Brazil remain hopeful he will be available for international duty.
Advertisement
The timing of these injuries presents an additional challenge for Madrid as they attempt to close the gap at the top of the table.
With several key fixtures still to come, squad depth will be tested in the absence of important players.
The focus for Guler will now shift towards recovery, with the priority being full fitness ahead of the World Cup.
While his club campaign appears to have been cut short, the possibility of featuring on the global stage is a realistic target.
